design-review.notes: /plan-eng-review pass 2026-07-05 (gstack skill invoked; gstack infra not installed in this repo, ran review content only). 4 findings: installer overwrite (accepted, matches existing behavior), artifact namespacing (fixed, workspace.md updated), T9 renumber fragility (fixed, runbook now has full corrected blocks), missing spine-consistency test (fixed, added T9.5). Scope-complexity heuristic triggered (20+ files, 2 new services) but user confirmed proceed-as-locked given 3x prior correction + Discovery-track decide-build already passed.

G1.notes: STRIDE applied to the 2 real data flows: (A) sub-agent JSON output -> discovery.json write — Tampering risk (malformed-but-non-empty JSON) partially mitigated by T9's non-empty gate, residual risk = idea-002 follow-up #1 (add shape/type check), not a blocker; all other STRIDE categories N/A (local session, no auth boundary, no network, no multi-user attribution). (B) manifest -> dist/release.zip -> install.sh -> user .claude/agents/ — Tampering finding: new agents-install section does unconditional cp with no existing-file guard, matching the installer's existing (unchanged) behavior for skills — not a novel gap introduced by this epic, noted for future consideration rather than blocking. Privacy: no PII collected/stored/transmitted anywhere in this epic. Auth: N/A, local CLI, no new auth boundary. Blast-radius: confined to dist/.release-build (build-local), .claude/ via dogfood (guarded by assertInsideClaude()), and <project>/.claude/agents/ via installer (same boundary the installer already writes within for skills/hooks/commands). Never-automate list: .agents/ts-deliver-router/artifacts/EPIC-DUAL-TRACK-ORCHESTRATION/think/never_automate.md (8 items + recovery paths). human_sign_off still required from ctony before Think->Plan exit.

staff-review.notes: gstack /code-review high, 8 angles, 3 findings (2 fixed commit 7e7024d, 1 deferred non-blocking). See review/report.md.

security-review.notes: No new attack surface vs G1 STRIDE analysis. Installer cp-overwrite behavior unchanged from pre-existing skills/hooks pattern.

G2.notes: No new attack surface: CLI/installer scripts only, no network/auth boundary. install.sh/.ps1 agents block reuses existing hooks-block JSON-parse pattern (no new injection risk). Entry points (manifest.json, discovery.json reads) use safe fallbacks, verified no crash on missing keys. Residual risk (sub-agent output has no shape/type validation, only non-empty check) already accepted at G1, tracked as idea-002 follow-up #1, not novel to Ship. Rollback path (npm run dogfood:rollback) live-tested, correctly restores/re-syncs agents category. Installer cp-overwrite is existing irreversible behavior extended to a 3rd category, not new. Signed off by ctony after item-by-item review.

plan.notes: Spectra:propose done via /spectra-propose: proposal, design, tasks written (specs skipped — zero capability deltas, pure internal refactor; analyzer 0 findings, spectra validate passes with expected 'no delta specs' warning). /plan-eng-review run: Step 0 scope challenge passed (4 files, 0 new services, no STOP). Architecture: no findings. Code Quality: 1 finding (bash read field-width footgun) fixed. Test: 1 finding (missing unit tests for 2 new pure helpers) fixed, tasks 2.3/3.3 added. Performance: no findings. Outside-voice (Codex unavailable, Claude subagent fallback) surfaced 4 more real gaps, all fixed: unsafe ps1 -WhatIf 'syntax check' that would actually live-run the installer (task 5.1 corrected to PSParser tokenize), missing default/unknown-tag arm in both installers' merged dispatch (added, fail-loud), dir-precreation placement unaddressed (pinned explicit in tasks 4.2/5.1), stdout wording/ordering not captured in regression diffing (added to tasks 1.1/6.1). Re-validated clean after every fix. Change parked.

build.notes: /spectra-apply, all 12 tasks done. dogfood.mjs: pushCategoryTargets extracted (moved to new scripts/lib/dedup-helpers.mjs, matching exclusions.mjs/golden-templates.mjs convention, for testability — design refinement, srcField option added for --from-zip's dest-not-src source resolution, a real bug caught mid-implementation and fixed). build-release.mjs: copyManifestCategory extracted to same lib file, validateSkill throw + missing-source-warning asymmetry preserved exactly. install.sh: consolidated to 1 python heredoc + 1 tagged while/case loop with fail-loud unknown-tag arm; header-ordering conflict (single loop vs interleaved per-category headers) surfaced mid-Build, resolved via user AskUserQuestion (chose: hoist 3 headers up front, same text, documented exception). install.ps1: mirrored via $InstallActions scriptblock hashtable + ContainsKey guard. New scripts/tests/dedup-helpers.test.mjs (14 tests). Every task individually verified against live baseline (not just code review): mirrorTargets()/--from-zip JSON diffs, validateSkill throw test (real missing-SKILL.md run), zip-listing/stdout diffs vs captured baseline, unknown-tag fixture tests (both sh case and ps1 ContainsKey), real pwsh 7.6.3 smoke install matching sh-side tree (67 paths). Full regression: npm run release/pilot/dogfood/verify-install all pass, all diffs empty (pilot stdout diff = only random fixture tmpdir path, expected noise). Full vitest suite 100/100 pass, type-check clean. Commit not yet made — scoped diff ready (release/install.sh, release/install.ps1, scripts/build-release.mjs, scripts/dogfood.mjs, scripts/lib/dedup-helpers.mjs, scripts/tests/dedup-helpers.test.mjs); repo has pre-existing unrelated uncommitted changes from before this epic that must not be bundled in.
